textPlain: When they sent the voyagers out, they had plaques that represented the species, with an attempt at universally communicating who we are. These ones have the equivalent of spray painted tags, or a name scratched into a desk: “John woz ere”. Different times.
To an archeologist both artifacts are worth having, just look at Pompeii the frescoes tell you alot but the graffiti on the sides of the buildings tells you something as well.
These things are only ("only"!) going to Mars, anybody getting that close to us and wanting to know more can go right to the source.

Not that it changes your point, but the plaques were on the Pioneer probes; Voyager had the golden record.https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Voyager_Golden_Record
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pioneer_plaque
This strikes me as a rather uncharitable view. I think it's okay for people to be proud of their work on a difficult project, and want to have their names on it.
Those voyager plaques will last millions, even billions of years.The records might survive to the end of time if they are lucky and get flung into intergalactic space post andromeda collision.
They just don't make em like they used to!yells at cloud
